mnemonics.title: TBL Pillars
mnemonics.content: Think of TBL as the three-legged stool: People (social responsibility), Planet (environmental care), and Profit (economic survival) - all three legs are needed for the stool to stand.

mnemonics.title: Green IT Benefits
mnemonics.content: GREEN IT: G-Energy savings, R-Resource efficiency, E-Environmental compliance, E-E-waste reduction, N-No reputation damage, I-Innovation, T-Tax benefits.

mnemonics.title: PUE Formula
mnemonics.content: PUE = Total Facility Power ÷ IT Equipment Power. Remember: PUE = 1 means Perfect Use of Energy - all power goes to computing!

mnemonics.title: LCA Stages
mnemonics.content: LCA = Earth's Materials Extracted → Manufacturing → Usage → Disposal. Think: Every Material Eventually Disappears.

acronyms.usage: Framework measuring People, Planet, and Profit for sustainability assessment
Complete lifecycle costs including acquisition, operation, and disposal
Method to evaluate environmental impacts from cradle to grave
Policy requiring manufacturers to manage end-of-life product disposal
Financial return measurement for green IT projects

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a pizza (total energy) where only the cheese and toppings (IT equipment) do useful work. The crust (cooling, lighting) supports but isn't directly productive. Perfect pizza = all toppings!
Draw three overlapping circles forming a central intersection. The overlap represents sustainable development where all three dimensions work together harmoniously.
Think of hot aisle/cold aisle containment like refrigerator organization - keeping cold items separated from hot items prevents energy waste from mixing temperatures.

visualTips.visualize: Balance scale with 'Environmental Impact' on one side and 'Financial Returns' on the other. Green IT seeks equilibrium - both sides should be balanced for sustainable decisions.

analogies.analogy: Like one apartment building (physical server) with many individual apartments (virtual machines) - more efficient use of space and resources than building many separate houses.
Like hazardous household waste (batteries, paints) - needs special handling because improper disposal contaminates the environment and harms health.
Like a dietary diary for emissions - just as calories track food intake, carbon footprint tracks greenhouse gas emissions contributing to climate change.
Like a household budget but for environmental costs - tracking 'spending' on energy, waste disposal, and pollution control to manage resources wisely.
